news_items.title: Brazil XI vs Haiti: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today - Yahoo Sports
news_items.author: Oli Gent Fri, 19 June 2026 at 11:08 am UTC · 2 min read
news_items.text:
Brazil XI vs Haiti: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today - Yahoo Sports

Advertisement

Advertisement

Advertisement

Advertisement

Story by

Brazil XI vs Haiti: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today

Brazil will remain without Neymar as they continue their World Cup campaign against Haiti tonight.

The Selecao hoped that their veteran star would miss just the one match of their summer tournament after he suffered a calf injury in the days leading up, but it has been revealed that he will remain sidelined once more for their matchday two encounter with Haiti.

Advertisement

Advertisement

As such, it shouldn’t change Carlo Ancelotti’s side too much.

Brazil were held to a 1-1 draw by Morocco last time out, and they were dominated for large periods of the encounter in East Rutherford, New Jersey.

Star man Vinicius Jr popped up with a stunning equaliser to snatch a point, but it was a game that most Brazil fans would rather forget.

Now, there are decisions to be made for Ancelotti, who surprised many with his team selection in the opener.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Injured: Neymar (Getty)

That was particu

# Brazil vs. Haiti projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 Group C game in Philadelphia

Marios Hamboullas

•

SN

Add The Sporting News on Google

Brazil and Haiti meet on Friday in Philadelphia in a World Cup Group C game that could have significant implications in the race to reach the knockout phase.

The Selecao were held to a surprising 1-1 draw by Morocco on matchday one, while Haiti suffered a narrow 1-0 defeat to Scotland despite a spirited performance.

That leaves Brazil looking for their first win of the tournament

# Brazil vs. Haiti projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 Group C game in Philadelphia

Marios Hamboullas

•

SN

Add The Sporting News on Google

Brazil and Haiti meet on Friday in Philadelphia in a World Cup Group C game that could have significant implications in the race to reach the knockout phase.

The Selecao were held to a surprising 1-1 draw by Morocco on matchday one, while Haiti suffered a narrow 1-0 defeat to Scotland despite a spirited performance.

That leaves Brazil looking for their first win of the tournament

news_items.title: Neymar won't play for Brazil vs. Haiti in race to be fit for World Cup knockout stages - ESPN
news_items.author: ESPN News Services
news_items.text:
Neymar won't play for Brazil vs. Haiti in race to be fit for World Cup knockout stages - ESPN

Skip to main content Skip to navigation

play

Neymar will not play or travel with the Brazil team to Philadelphia for their World Cup Group C match against Haiti, the Brazil federation confirmed on Thursday.

"He will remain in New Jersey to optimize the final phase of his recovery process, making use of the excellent facilities at The Ridge hotel and the Columbia Park training center," the CBF said of the Red Bull New York facility in a news release.

Neymar, 34, who has been out for a month with a right calf injury, trained in uniform and joined his Brazil teammates briefly for the first time during this World Cup at the club's practice Wednesday in Morristown, New Jersey.

He came out onto the pitch on Wednesday along with Raphinha, Bruno Guimarães, Gabriel Magalhães and Fabinho, and joked with members of the media, saying "Did you miss me?"

After some initial work with the team, Neymar moved back to the sideline to continue with individual work alongside Brazil team coaches.

He had also trained alone on the sideline Tuesday for the first time since arriving in the United States.

T

Neymar will not play or travel with the Brazil team to Philadelphia for their World Cup Group C match against Haiti, the Brazil federation confirmed on Thursday.

"He will remain in New Jersey to optimize the final phase of his recovery process, making use of the excellent facilities at The Ridge hotel and the Columbia Park training center," the CBF said of the Red Bull New York facility in a news release.

Neymar, 34, who has been out for a month with a right calf injury, trained in uniform and joined his Brazil teammates briefly for the first time during this World Cup at the club's practice Wednesday in Morristown, New Jersey.

He came out onto the pitch on Wednesday along with Raphinha, Bruno Guimarães, Gabriel Magalhães and Fabinho, and joked with members of the media, saying "Did you miss me?"

After some initial work with the team, Neymar moved back to the sideline to continue with individual work alongside Brazil team coaches.

He had also trained alone on the sideline Tuesday for the first time since arriving in the United States.

The 2026 World Cup will have 13 different kickoff times. You can use the Al Jazeera Sport widget to find out exactly when your team is playing in your local time.

# Brazil’s Possible Lineup to Face Haiti at the FIFA World Cup 2026

## Carlo Ancelotti’s side needs a win after starting with a draw against Morocco. How will they fare against the CONCACAF team?

The Brazil national team is in Philadelphia, where it will look for its first victory at the FIFA World Cup 2026 against a seemingly manageable opponent in Haiti.

This Friday, June 19, they will face the CONCACAF side after drawing 1-1 with Morocco in their opening match of the tournament. That is why we bring you the possible lineup Carlo Ancelotti could field for this encounter.

Brazil XI vs Haiti: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today

Brazil will remain without Neymar as they continue their World Cup campaign against Haiti tonight.

The Selecao hoped that their veteran star would miss just the one match of their summer tournament after he suffered a calf injury in the days leading up, but it has been revealed that he will remain sidelined once more for their matchday two encounter with Haiti.

Advertisement

Advertisement

As such, it shouldn’t change Carlo Ancelotti’s side too much.

Brazil were held to a 1-1 draw by Morocco last time out, and they were dominated for large periods of the encounter in East Rutherford, New Jersey.

Star man Vinicius Jr popped up with a stunning equaliser to snatch a point, but it was a game that most Brazil fans would rather forget.

Now, there are decisions to be made for Ancelotti, who surprised many with his team selection in the opener.

# Brazil vs. Haiti projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 Group C game in Philadelphia

Marios Hamboullas

•

SN

Add The Sporting News on Google

Brazil and Haiti meet on Friday in Philadelphia in a World Cup Group C game that could have significant implications in the race to reach the knockout phase.

The Selecao were held to a surprising 1-1 draw by Morocco on matchday one, while Haiti suffered a narrow 1-0 defeat to Scotland despite a spirited performance.

That leaves Brazil looking for their first win of the tournament"}
{"title": "Neymar won't play for Brazil vs. Haiti in race to be fit for World Cup knockout stages - ESPN", "url": "https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/49107492/neymar-play-brazil-vs-haiti-race-fit-world-cup-knockout-stages", "publishedDate": "2026-06-18T14:58:00.000Z", "author": "ESPN News Services", "text": "Neymar won't play for Brazil vs. Haiti in race to be fit for World Cup knockout stages - ESPN

Skip to main content Skip to navigation

play

Neymar will not play or travel with the Brazil team to Philadelphia for their World Cup Group C match against Haiti, the Brazil federation confirmed on Thursday.

"He will remain in New Jersey to optimize the final phase of his recovery process, making use of the excellent facilities at The Ridge hotel and the Columbia Park training center," the CBF said of the Red Bull New York facility in a news release.

Neymar, 34, who has been out for a month with a right calf injury, trained in uniform and joined his Brazil teammates briefly for the first time during this World Cup at the club's practice Wednesday in Morristown, New Jersey.

He came out onto the pitch on Wednesday along with Raphinha, Bruno Guimarães, Gabriel Magalhães and Fabinho, and joked with members of the media, saying "Did you miss me?"

After some initial work with the team, Neymar moved back to the sideline to continue with individual work alongside Brazil team coaches.

He had also trained alone on the sideline Tuesday for the first time since arriving in the United States.

T"}

# Brazil’s Possible Lineup to Face Haiti at the FIFA World Cup 2026

## Carlo Ancelotti’s side needs a win after starting with a draw against Morocco. How will they fare against the CONCACAF team?

The Brazil national team is in Philadelphia, where it will look for its first victory at the FIFA World Cup 2026 against a seemingly manageable opponent in Haiti.

This Friday, June 19, they will face the CONCACAF side after drawing 1-1 with Morocco in their opening match of the tournament. That is why we bring you the possible lineup Carlo Ancelotti could field for this encounter.

## THE BET AND A MIDFIELD SURPRISE
